title: Move sub items from an item to another
excerpt: Impossible to duplicate items with sub-items and checklists in the subitems. One way would be to duplicate the subitems (with checklists) and move them to another element.

posts.author: MediReport (Eric Eisenberg)
posts.cookedHtml: Impossible to duplicate items with sub-items and checklists in the subitems. One way would be to duplicate the subitems (with checklists) and move them to another element.

posts.author: CandyJE (Candy Johnson-Eich)
posts.cookedHtml:
I need to be able to move sub-items from one parent to another, across groups.
We do a lot of pre-planning across 20-30 programs, and it’s really cumbersome to have to re-create sub-items when they need to be moved from our forecast to our queue, and then into our active work.
Please add in the ability to move sub-items! Thank you!

posts.author: serena.LOC.com (Serena)
posts.cookedHtml: We have a Main Project board with each pulse having about 30 subitems. Those are then assigned to 10 different people. I need to be able to duplicate subitems and move them to another board where each Employee has their own Group. I do not currently see a way to do that.

posts.author: MikeC (Mike Corbitt)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a significantly desired feature for managing projects through monday.

posts.author: MonDude (MonDude)
posts.cookedHtml: Definitely need this, this is major.

posts.author: MonDude (MonDude)
posts.cookedHtml: If you can’t move sub items, it would at least be good to “make a copy for _____” and you can choose the parent task from others on the board.

posts.author: michelle.tricker (Michelle Tricker)
posts.cookedHtml: This is absolutely needed please

posts.author: mirving (Michael Irving)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely critical for workflow. Even linking or copying won’t work because we may have conversation history tied to the subitem

posts.author: SimonEvans (Simon Evans)
posts.cookedHtml: Absolutely agree - this is a critical need.

posts.author: llagos (Leo Lagos)
posts.cookedHtml: We also need this…! Urgently…!

posts.author: anchee (AC)
posts.cookedHtml: This is substantial for my project as well

posts.author: NickiN (Nicole)
posts.cookedHtml:
Has anyone figured out a way around copying/duplicating subitems to existing boards?

I need to add a new Subitem on each staff memeber’s board for them to advise availability (see pic) and cannot figure out how to do it without recreating on each board. Don’t really feel like recreating over 150 times!

Thanks team!

Availability subitem1538×326 29.8 KB

posts.author: MediReport (Eric Eisenberg)
posts.cookedHtml:
I asked for that feature months ago. I have a group of model elements with different subitems, and I can’t duplicate them !

Dicté à mon iPhone

posts.author: reTyre (Tamara Rosic)
posts.cookedHtml:
Any update on this? Can we at least get an ETA, or if it’s planned at all??!

Envisioned as such a cool feature, but it’s lacking basic functionality, please work on this!

posts.author: ShannaKaeM (Shanna)
posts.cookedHtml: Major need for this! I am a new user and i have things set up where this feature would be very important. I am cool leaving things as they are for now, as long as i know this feature is in the works. Any update on the status of this feature would be much appreciated. Thanks so much, i am really enjoying Monday so far, this would be an amazing feature addition.

posts.author: erin_de (erin demarco)
posts.cookedHtml: I want to drag my sub item from the initial task I put it under to a different one because I accidentally put it in the wrong one. How do I just drag it to the correct parent?

posts.author: AZQA (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml:
This is a must have, and seems like a major oversight. How are we supposed to use subitems when they are locked into the item?

Usecase: Task has 10 subitems. 9 subitems are completed, but 10th needs to be backlogged. As a user I want to move this subitem to a new group, to be completed at a later date.

Usecase: As a user I want to move a subitem from one task to another, so I can assign it to a different team.

posts.author: Donna_F (Donna Furniss)
posts.cookedHtml: Very interested in an update on this, would be a massive help in setting up and managing projects

posts.author: dgowan (Dave Gowan)
posts.cookedHtml: this is exactly what I am looking for… sub-item seems pointless unless this is an option.

posts.author: Randi1 (Randi Fuchs)
posts.cookedHtml:
Ditto to everything mentioned above! Moving around subitems gives users the flexibility to customize their workflow and make adjustments to timelines - something that monday claims is easy to do…

Another odd thing about subitem functionality is that when I select it, it gives me the option to copy/paste, but that just doesn’t work at all. Why is that option there if it doesn’t work?

posts.author: tina (Tina)
posts.cookedHtml: Has anyone seen an update or reply to moving subitems to another item? Just hit this roadblock today.

posts.author: tsterling (Tanya Sterling)
posts.cookedHtml: This would be very useful for us as well. Thank you

posts.author: mildorviolon (Jean-Francois McDonald)
posts.cookedHtml: That would be super useful and very easy to implement, just look at Mac and Windows folders, its just like draging and droping a file from a folder to another one…

posts.author: Jackie.Lee (Jackie Lee)
posts.cookedHtml: This is noted as released in the Subitems roadmap. Does anyone know if it actually was or if there is something which needs to be addressed in order to allow it?

posts.author: samtoms (Sam Toms)
posts.cookedHtml: I just tested it out and it works on my account Jackie. I just selected the sub item like I would any pulse and the move option was available, you then choose the pulse where you would prefer it to be.

posts.author: adibar (adi)
posts.cookedHtml:
I can also see it is possible now to move sub item from one item to another in the same board
I think it is also crucial to be able to:

  • move sub item also between boards
  • be able to convert item to a sub item and the opposite

posts.author: gma (Gilda M)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello! I can move subitems now but only by right clicking and choosing the “Move” option. Is it possible to have this feature work via drag and drop?
It’s a step in the right direction though - thanks for finally getting on this!

posts.author: Ozo (Jessica Santascoy)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday.com I need this feature right now. Or a work around. It makes me look inefficient as a PM when I have to wait for a feature to be built. What is the ETA for this feature?

posts.author: Ozo (Jessica Santascoy)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree. Please, monday.com team move quickly on this. Other pm software programs have this feature as a basic feature. Even OmniFocus, which is for personal management has this feature. Please get this done ASAP!

posts.author: Aquifer (Nat Pierson)
posts.cookedHtml: @monday.com: this feature request seems pretty reasonable, I was pretty disappointed and puzzled to find this is not possible.

posts.author: AZQA (Justin)
posts.cookedHtml: It’s one of many obvious things that MDC lacks. We are finding this software to be nearly unusable.

posts.author: brett-monday.com (Brett Dalton)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi all,

We actually have released this functionality to all accounts on our platform to move subitems from item to item within the same board!

It can be accessed via the subitem dropdown menu:

image984×1222 94.7 KB

I hope this helps

Cheers,
Brett

posts.author: system (system)
posts.cookedHtml: This topic was automatically closed 7 days after the last reply. New replies are no longer allowed.
